verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: Guard test written; Runs in CI; Fails on regression; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gates executed red with the guard script absent (spec failing), green at HEAD (3/3)
gate_result.guard_evidence: scripts/check-durable-not-on-run.mjs enforces invariant I06 in two honest layers: default mode FAILS on any durable tier-map entry whose TARGET is on /run (configuration regression) while openly reporting the known restart-gated live misplacement; --strict additionally fails on live misplacement (the post-migration CI mode).
gate_result.ci_evidence: The guard executes inside the repository's vitest gate (tests/archbp-075-durable-run-guard.spec.ts), which runs in every full-suite invocation — default mode passes today on clean targets while stderr reports the CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/CODEX_HOME misplacement.
gate_result.regression_evidence: A negative fixture with a durable entry targeting /run/user/1001/oops makes the guard exit 1 with a REGRESSION line; strict mode exits 1 today, proving the live-misplacement detector is armed for the post-migration flip.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: The guard runs live against the real tier map and a real negative fixture in CI.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-075 is complete: the CI guard fails whenever any durable var targets host /run — proven with a rejected regression fixture — runs inside the repository's always-on vitest gate, honestly reports the current restart-gated misplacement instead of hiding it, and carries a strict mode ready to hard-fail once the T3 migration retires the tmpfs residents.
